rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY TAYLOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for the judicial officer to review and determine whether this petition should be denied without prejudice or taken off calendar, in light of the administrator's 03/09/2026 "Status Report" disclosing some additional tasks preventing this estate from closing and rendering the subject petition "for final distribution" premature, with the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 11/21/2025) unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) The 2012 Toyota Highlander must be appraised by a probate referee. 3) TIAA-CREF retirement account, if not distributed in a lump sum to the estate, must be appraised by a probate referee. 4) Once a corrected inventory and appraisal with a new probate referee's signature is filed, statutory fees payable to petitioner and his attorney and net cash amount distributable to each beneficiary must be recalculated. 5) The account period exceeds 2 years from the date of death. No court approval has been obtained or sought to exceed the 2-year period. LRSF 14.34(A). 6) If the ending date of the first account is 08/31/2024, the first account is treated as an interim account (and not as a final account). Absent waivers of final account signed by all other beneficiaries, the ending date of the final account must be close to the filing date of this petition. 7) Each interest and gain credited to TIAA-CREF account must be separately stated on the schedule of receipts (date and amount of each payment). 8) The statutory fee base receipts ($88,940.69) must be reduced by $100 sought to be reimbursed to petitioner. The statutory fees and net cash amount distributable to each beneficiary must be recalculated. 9) Consents signed by all beneficiaries must be filed to request non-pro rata distribution of the assets on hand. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a)(ii). 10) Petitioner's bond is insufficient to cover the market value of the assets on hand. An ex parte petition must be filed to increase the bond, absent additional bond waivers signed by all other beneficiaries. 11) A statement of each financial account on hand covering 08/31/2024 must be filed. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention and compliance with inspection of each original account statement required by LRSF 14.59(A) and CRC 2.257 must also be filed. 12) Please explain the purpose of "litigation support" for which the attorney paid $50 to Montoya & Associates and is seeking reimbursement from the estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO CANALES BECERRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Spousal Property Petition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: (1) Petitioner alleges that her 2014 Transfer Trust severed the joint tenancy. The petition is silent on whether the transaction was made with the spouse's written consent during the marriage. (2) If the transfer was valid, what legal authority allows the Court to confirm a one-half interest in real property that was already given to the surviving spouse in 2014? It remains unclear how the Court could claim jurisdiction over property now held by a trust, not by the surviving spouse as an individual. (3) Petition to set forth all known heirs of the decedent. Sec.13651(a)(5). (4) Notice of hearing must be given to those heirs. Sec. 13655(a)(2). Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. (5) Petition #3 to set forth the date of death. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN M. BAIRD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/22/2026. All of the following issues must be cleared two weeks before the hearing: (1) The original will has not yet been submitted, nor has the petition been updated to request admission of a lost will. While the petitioner filed a declaration regarding the original will on 2/26/2026, it does not seem to support the petition. To move forward, the petitioner must submit the original will if seeking its admission. If, instead, the petitioner wishes to admit a lost will, an amended petition with the necessary details will likely be required. (2) According to the supplement, Scott H. appointed Joseph B. and Cassandra S. as executors. Unless written refusals from both are provided, the Court lacks clear authority to appoint the petitioner in their place. (3) The petition fails to name the trustee of the residual beneficiary trust, and proper notice to the trustee is required. Notice on file lists several beneficiaries; however, no addresses are listed for Robert B., Cassandra S., and Marisa Leonis. (4) The petition needs to clarify how the petitioner plans to resolve the conflict of interest arising from their role as a creditor. (5) Because the estimated estate value falls below the $208,850 threshold for someone who died in March 2026, it is still unclear if probate is necessary. (6) Even if all outstanding issues are resolved, the Court is unlikely to waive the bond requirement.
